title: First Coal
description: A solo frame drum strikes the first dry, resonant beats over still air as a single coal catches; deep house bass enters last, low and patient, to seal the circle.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (solo frame drum, room tone, distant night air) -> Build 1 (second frame drum joins in call-and-response, sub bass pulse arrives) -> Drop 1 (soft tribal house groove, brushed hi-hat, cinematic pad bed) -> Breakdown (frame drum solo returns, pad swells alone) -> Outro (drums thin to single frame drum, fade to room tone)
soundDesignPrompt: Lead voice is a goat-skin frame drum (close-miked, dry, fingertip rolls and low palm strikes) trading phrases with a second frame drum tuned a fourth lower; bass is a rounded deep house sine-sub with soft saturation, never aggressive; pad is a slow-moving cinematic string-and-air texture sitting far back, barely above the noise floor.
mixMasterNotes: Keep the frame drum transient sharp and intimate in mono-center, let the sub bass sit wide and felt rather than heard, leave generous headroom so the track breathes quietly before the album builds — this is the quietest moment on the record.
djTransitionNotes: Long ambient intro (32 bars) makes this ideal as an opening set piece or a slow blend in from silence; mixes cleanly into track 2 at the same BPM, no drop to navigate, exit on the bare frame-drum outro.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, minimal hand percussion, solo frame drum call-and-response, soft deep house sub bass, distant cinematic pad, 119 BPM, warm nocturnal gathering mood, slow patient build, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A single ember-gold point of light pulses on a black field with each frame drum strike, slowly joined by a second point in deep-earth-brown that orbits it; night-fire-red haze breathes in time with the sub bass; motion stays still and vertical, like smoke rising.
filmPrompt: Static wide shot, firelight just catching at frame edge, slow creep toward a single hand resting on drum skin; smoke rises dead straight in still air; no faces, just texture and heat-shimmer.
imagePrompt: A single goat-skin frame drum resting upright on packed earth beside a newly lit coal bed, one hand mid-strike at the edge of frame, firelight catching gold dust in the still air, deep earth-brown shadows, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, no visible faces, respectful and quiet.

title: Circle of Hands
description: Multiple hand drums enter in a layered round, each voice taking a different pocket of the beat, while a warm deep house bassline starts to walk underneath.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (congas alone, loose and conversational) -> Build 1 (bongos layer in higher register, bass walks in) -> Drop 1 (full hand-drum round locks into groove, pad arrives) -> Breakdown (congas drop to two players, pad solo) -> Build 2 (full round returns, filter opens) -> Outro (drums thin to congas, bass fades under)
soundDesignPrompt: Three conga voices (tumba, conga, quinto) interlock in a traditional round-robin pattern, each panned to a distinct position to suggest physical bodies in a circle; bongos add a brighter top-register accent on the offbeat; bass is a walking deep house line with a rubbery, slightly detuned character; pad is warm analog strings with slow filter movement.
mixMasterNotes: Prioritize stereo separation between the three conga voices so each hand is distinguishable in a club system; bass should be felt through the chest without masking the conga low end; keep transients punchy but not harsh for outdoor PA translation.
djTransitionNotes: 120 BPM sits centrally in the album range, easy two-way mix with tracks 1 and 3; breakdown at the midpoint gives a natural 16-bar window for a beat-matched blend; outro conga-only tail extends nicely under an incoming track's intro.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, layered conga and bongo round, interlocking hand percussion, warm walking deep house bass, analog string pad, 120 BPM, communal gathering mood, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Three rings of ember-orange light rotate at different speeds around a shared center, each ring pulsing to its own conga voice; ritual-gold particles drift outward on the bass hits; background deep-earth-brown gradient breathes slowly with the pad.
filmPrompt: Slow circular dolly around a ring of drums on stands, firelight flickering across worn drum skins, shallow depth of field, smoke drifting between frames, no faces shown.
imagePrompt: Three conga drums of varying heights arranged in a loose circle on packed earth at dusk, firelight reflecting off worn leather skins, gold dust suspended in the air between them, warm earth-brown and ember-orange tones, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, no people, respectful and grounded.

title: Embercall
description: A high, bright frame drum pattern calls out over a slow-building cinematic pad, with the deep house bass finally settling into a steady four-on-the-floor pulse to close the gathering phase.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (bright frame drum pattern, sparse pad swell) -> Build 1 (low toms enter under the pattern, bass pulse steadies) -> Drop 1 (four-on-the-floor locks in, full percussion ensemble) -> Breakdown (pad and distant Portuguese guitar texture alone) -> Build 2 (drums re-enter, filter sweep) -> Outro (bass and pad fade, frame drum pattern returns solo)
soundDesignPrompt: Lead is a small high-pitched frame drum (riq-style jingles muted, struck only) carrying a bright, quick pattern; low toms provide weight underneath in a simple rolling figure; bass shifts here to a steady deep house four-on-the-floor pulse with a soft sidechain pump; pad is widescreen cinematic strings with a single Portuguese guitar phrase appearing once, deep in the mix, as pure atmosphere.
mixMasterNotes: Let the bright frame drum cut through clearly in the upper-mid without becoming harsh; the guitar texture should be felt more than heard, treated with reverb and low-pass filtering so it never reads as a melodic lead; bass pulse should sit tight and controlled for sustained club playback.
djTransitionNotes: Closes the fire-lit phase, so works well mixed out into track 4's rising energy; the four-on-the-floor pulse makes beat-matching straightforward; breakdown section (bars 33-48) is the cleanest entry/exit point for a DJ blend.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, bright frame drum lead, rolling low toms, steady four-on-the-floor deep house bass, cinematic widescreen pad, single distant Portuguese guitar texture, 121 BPM, anticipatory warm mood, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A bright ritual-gold spark pattern flickers in quick bursts against a slow-pulsing night-fire-red backdrop; low rolling shapes in deep earth-brown roll across the bottom of frame in sync with the toms; a single thin gold thread appears once during the breakdown and dissolves.
filmPrompt: Low-angle slow push toward rising sparks against a dark sky, embers tracking upward in soft motion blur, single out-of-focus silhouette of a guitar resting against a drum case in the background, no faces.
imagePrompt: Sparks rising from a fire pit against a deep night sky, a small bright frame drum mid-strike in foreground silhouette, an out-of-focus Portuguese guitar leaning against a drum case in the background, gold dust and ember-orange motion-blur, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, respectful and atmospheric.

title: Tomline
description: Rolling toms drive a propulsive new rhythm as the deep house bass thickens, marking the moment the gathering turns into collective movement.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (rolling tom pattern alone, filtered) -> Build 1 (hi-hat and shaker enter, bass thickens) -> Drop 1 (full tribal house groove, congas accent the toms) -> Breakdown (toms solo, pad swell) -> Build 2 (full groove returns with added bongo fills) -> Outro (toms and bass loop down, hi-hat fades)
soundDesignPrompt: Lead rhythm is a set of three tuned toms (low-mid-high) playing a rolling, propulsive figure reminiscent of a moving procession; congas add syncopated accents around the tom pattern rather than competing with it; bass is a thicker deep house growl with light analog distortion; pad is a slow cinematic swell that only fully blooms in the breakdown.
mixMasterNotes: Tom tuning should be clearly pitched and distinct from the bass register to avoid low-end clutter; keep the rolling tom pattern forward and present as the rhythmic anchor; bass growl should add warmth without muddying the toms underneath.
djTransitionNotes: First track of the rising phase — pairs naturally with track 3's outro and track 5's intro at adjacent BPMs; tom-solo breakdown around bar 49 offers a strong cue point for layering in a second track's percussion.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, rolling tuned toms, syncopated conga accents, thickened deep house bass growl, cinematic pad swell, 121 BPM, propulsive collective-movement mood, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Three horizontal bands in night-fire-red, ember-orange, and ritual-gold roll across the frame like waves, each tied to one tom voice; particles accelerate and scatter on the conga accents; the whole frame surges forward in a slow, weighted pulse.
filmPrompt: Tracking shot moving low and fast alongside a line of drums lit by firelight, motion blur on gold dust kicked up from packed earth, rhythmic handheld sway, no faces in frame.
imagePrompt: A row of three tuned tom drums of ascending size on packed earth, firelight streaking across their skins in motion-blurred gold dust, deep earth-brown ground giving way to night-fire-red glow at the horizon, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, dynamic and respectful.

title: Quinto Call-and-Response
description: A high-pitched quinto conga improvises lead phrases against a steady supporting conga pattern, structured as a true call-and-response dialogue over a driving deep house groove.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (supporting conga pattern alone) -> Build 1 (quinto enters with call phrases, bass arrives) -> Drop 1 (full call-and-response locked with tribal house groove) -> Breakdown (quinto solo improvisation, pad and bass drop out) -> Build 2 (full groove re-enters, energy lifted) -> Outro (call-and-response resolves to supporting conga alone)
soundDesignPrompt: Two conga voices: a steady tumba holding the supporting pattern, and a high quinto improvising call phrases that genuinely vary across the track rather than looping; bass is a driving deep house line with a short, percussive pluck character that interlocks rhythmically with the congas rather than just holding root notes; pad is a tense, slow-rising cinematic texture used sparingly to leave room for the percussion dialogue.
mixMasterNotes: This track lives or dies on percussion clarity — keep both conga voices tightly defined with minimal overlap in EQ, give the quinto a touch of presence boost so its improvisation reads clearly in a loud room; bass pluck should be tight and short to avoid blurring the conga transients.
djTransitionNotes: The quinto solo breakdown is the standout moment to cue live or loop; 122 BPM bridges comfortably to both track 4 and track 6; best mixed out during the supporting-conga-only outro to avoid clashing solos with an incoming track.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, conga call-and-response, improvised quinto lead, steady supporting conga, driving plucky deep house bass, tense cinematic pad, 122 BPM, percussion-dialogue rising mood, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Two interlocking shapes — a sharp ritual-gold spike and a rounded ember-orange pulse — trade dominance across the frame in rhythmic dialogue; night-fire-red background flares briefly during the quinto solo; motion has a conversational back-and-forth rhythm.
filmPrompt: Close handheld shot alternating focus between two drums set close together, firelight catching motion-blurred hands at the edge of frame, quick rhythmic cuts matching the call-and-response, no faces.
imagePrompt: Two conga drums of different heights placed close together on packed earth, one taller and brighter-lit suggesting a leading voice, firelight and gold dust swirling between them in motion blur, deep earth-brown shadow contrast, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, respectful and dynamic.

title: Bassline Under Bronze Skin
description: A deep, rolling house bassline takes the spotlight while bongos and a frame drum weave a tight rhythmic lattice above it, balancing club low-end with intricate hand percussion detail.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (bassline alone, filtered low-pass) -> Build 1 (bongo lattice enters, filter opens) -> Drop 1 (full groove, frame drum threading through bongo pattern) -> Breakdown (bass solo with filter sweep, percussion drops out) -> Build 2 (percussion lattice returns layered) -> Outro (bass and percussion fade together, filtered down)
soundDesignPrompt: Bass is the lead voice here: a deep, rolling deep house bassline with a rounded sub layer and a slightly resonant filter character that moves throughout the track; bongos play a tight, interlocking sixteenth-note lattice; a single small frame drum threads short fills through the gaps in the bongo pattern; pad is minimal, just a low cinematic drone to support the bass register.
mixMasterNotes: Give the bass real room to breathe in the low-mid without competing with the bongo lattice's higher frequencies; the frame drum fills should peek through cleanly in the stereo field; master with club playback in mind — strong low end that still translates on a smaller PA.
djTransitionNotes: The bass-solo breakdown is a strong moment for a DJ to drop in an a cappella-free percussion loop from elsewhere on the album; 122 BPM keeps continuous flow with tracks 5 and 7; long filtered outro allows an extended blend.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, deep rolling bassline lead, tight bongo sixteenth-note lattice, frame drum fills, low cinematic drone, 122 BPM, bass-forward club mood, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A thick ember-orange waveform pulses across the lower third of frame, its width swelling with the bassline; a fine lattice of ritual-gold lines weaves above it in time with the bongos, occasionally interrupted by a single bright thread from the frame drum.
filmPrompt: Low slow-motion shot from ground level looking up through firelight and smoke, bass-driven pulse felt in the framing's subtle zoom breathing, no faces, heat distortion visible against the night sky.
imagePrompt: Ground-level view through rising smoke and firelight toward a night sky, a small frame drum and a pair of bongos faintly visible at the edge of the glow, deep earth-brown foreground silhouettes against ember-orange haze, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, moody and respectful.

title: The Gathering Pulse
description: The full hand-percussion ensemble locks together for the first time — congas, bongos, frame drums, and toms in one unified pulse — as the cinematic pads swell to signal the approach of the peak.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (full ensemble already established, no buildup) -> Section A (congas and toms trade the main groove) -> Lift 1 (pad swell begins, bongo fills increase) -> Peak Tease (brief filtered drop, all percussion held) -> Drop (full ensemble returns at maximum density for this phase) -> Breakdown (pad swell alone, single tom heartbeat) -> Outro (ensemble re-enters briefly then cuts to silence)
soundDesignPrompt: All percussion voices established so far — congas, bongos, frame drums, toms — combine into one dense, interlocking ensemble pattern with no single lead voice; bass is a sustained deep house pulse that holds steady to let the percussion arrangement carry the energy; pad is the widest cinematic swell on the album so far, building continuously toward the peak phase.
mixMasterNotes: Arrangement clarity matters more than EQ tricks here — each percussion voice needs its own rhythmic pocket so the ensemble reads as full without becoming a wash; pad swell should be allowed to dominate briefly in the breakdown without overwhelming the single tom heartbeat underneath it.
djTransitionNotes: Functions as the hinge into the peak phase — strong choice to mix in early during a set transition since the ensemble is already at full density from the first bar; the brief silence at the outro is a natural hard-cut point rather than a blend.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, full hand-percussion ensemble, congas bongos frame drums and toms interlocking, sustained deep house pulse, widescreen cinematic pad swell, 123 BPM, unified anticipatory mood, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: All previous visual motifs — rings, lattices, waveforms — converge into one dense, pulsing mass of night-fire-red and ritual-gold light filling the frame; the swelling pad is shown as a slow outward bloom of gold dust that nearly fills the screen before the breakdown clears it to one small ember.
filmPrompt: Wide slow crane shot rising above a full circle of drums and firelight, smoke pooling and rising together, the camera holds at the peak of the rise before the breakdown brings it back down to one glowing coal, no faces.
imagePrompt: An aerial-leaning wide view of a full circle of varied drums around a central fire, smoke and gold dust rising together into the night sky, night-fire-red glow radiating outward into deep earth-brown darkness, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, expansive and respectful.

title: Skin and Low End
description: The album's first true peak-energy drop pairs a relentless conga-and-tom groove with the heaviest deep house bass on the record, built for full-floor movement.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (heavy bass and full percussion already locked, minimal build) -> Drop 1 (peak groove, congas and toms at maximum interlock) -> Break (filtered breakdown, percussion only, bass cut) -> Re-entry (bass returns with added low-end weight) -> Drop 2 (peak groove intensified with bongo overdub) -> Outro (groove sustains then thins gradually)
soundDesignPrompt: Congas and toms interlock at their densest arrangement on the album, functioning almost as a single instrument; bass is the heaviest on the record — deep house low end with added harmonic weight and a tight sidechain pump that locks to the kick pulse; pad is reduced to a low, dark cinematic drone that supports rather than decorates; no bright or jingling percussion here, keeping the texture weighty and grounded.
mixMasterNotes: This is the loudest, densest track on the album — prioritize low-end control and punch for sustained club playback, ensure the conga/tom interlock stays defined even at high density, master with extra headroom checked against club system playback to avoid low-end buildup.
djTransitionNotes: Peak-phase anchor track — best played deep into a set rather than as an opener; the filtered break is the ideal moment for a DJ to bring in an acapella-free percussion loop or layer track 9's intro; 123 BPM keeps it locked with neighboring peak tracks.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, dense interlocking conga and tom groove, heaviest deep house sub bass, dark cinematic drone, filtered breakdown, 123 BPM, peak floor-filling intensity, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: The frame fills almost entirely with deep night-fire-red, pulsing hard and low with the bass; thick bands of ember-orange and ritual-gold percussion shapes hammer in tight unison; during the filtered break the color drains to near-black before flooding back at full saturation.
filmPrompt: Fast low tracking shot weaving between drum players' hands and firelight, motion blur heavy, brief slow-motion hold during the breakdown before snapping back to speed, no faces shown.
imagePrompt: A tightly packed cluster of conga and tom drums catching hard firelight, hands mid-strike rendered as motion-blurred gold dust trails, deep night-fire-red glow dominating the frame, dramatic shadow and heat haze, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, intense and respectful.

title: Gold Dust Rhythm
description: Shaker and frame drum textures sparkle over the peak groove like dust caught in firelight, adding a lighter, glittering layer above the heaviest bass on the album.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (shaker texture alone, sparse and fast) -> Build 1 (frame drum layers in, bass arrives heavy) -> Drop 1 (full peak groove with shimmering shaker top-line) -> Breakdown (shaker and frame drum duet, bass and pad drop) -> Build 2 (full groove returns, additional conga overdub) -> Outro (shaker fades last, alone)
soundDesignPrompt: Shaker (seed-pod or metal-bead character) provides a continuous fast, glittering texture across the entire track, the brightest and busiest rhythmic layer on the album; frame drum adds accented hits underneath the shaker; bass matches track 8's weight and heaviness to sustain peak energy; pad is bright and shimmering rather than dark, a contrast to track 8's drone.
mixMasterNotes: Shaker texture needs careful high-frequency management to stay sparkling rather than harsh or fatiguing over a full club playback; balance the brightness of the shaker against the heavy bass so neither masks the other; this track should feel like the album's brightest peak moment.
djTransitionNotes: Highest BPM on the album at the energy summit — ideal for the literal peak of a set; shaker-only outro is a clean, gentle exit even after maximum intensity, useful for a DJ pulling back toward track 10.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, glittering continuous shaker texture, accented frame drum, heavy peak deep house bass, bright shimmering pad, 124 BPM, sparkling high-energy peak mood, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Countless small ritual-gold particles swirl rapidly across the entire frame like dust in firelight, density increasing with the shaker's intensity; an ember-orange pulse beneath the particle field tracks the heavy bass; motion is fast, granular, and constantly shifting.
filmPrompt: Extreme close-up macro shot of gold dust and sparks swirling in firelight and motion blur, fast handheld energy, cuts timed to the shaker pattern, no faces, abstract and textural throughout.
imagePrompt: Extreme close-up of gold dust and sparks suspended in firelight against a dark background, fine particles in fast motion blur suggesting shaken percussion, ember-orange glow at the frame edges, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, vivid and abstract.

title: The Last Drop
description: The album's final and largest peak combines every percussion voice introduced so far into one climactic arrangement before the energy begins its descent toward the embers.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (full ensemble immediately present, no warmup) -> Drop 1 (climactic groove, every percussion voice from the album combined) -> Breakdown (long cinematic pad swell, single conga heartbeat) -> Build (ensemble rebuilds layer by layer, drum by drum) -> Drop 2 (largest and final peak, maximum density) -> Outro (sudden thinning, only frame drum and bass remain)
soundDesignPrompt: Every percussion voice from the album — congas, bongos, frame drums, toms, shaker — combines in a single climactic arrangement, each given a clear rhythmic pocket through careful pattern design rather than just stacking volume; bass is full and weighty, matching the peak phase; pad swell in the breakdown is the longest and broadest on the album, a clear emotional high point before the descent begins.
mixMasterNotes: With this many percussion voices active, arrangement and panning discipline are essential — verify the mix translates on both a club system and a smaller speaker without collapsing into noise; the breakdown pad swell should be mastered with extra dynamic headroom to land emotionally.
djTransitionNotes: The natural set-peak closer — best placed at the very top of energy before pulling a set down; the long breakdown offers an extended window for a DJ to either hold the room or begin transitioning toward the embers phase; sudden thin outro signals a clear turning point.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, full combined percussion ensemble climax, congas bongos frame drums toms and shaker together, weighty deep house bass, longest cinematic pad swell, 122 BPM, climactic powerful peak mood, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Every prior visual motif converges at once — rings, lattices, particles, waveforms — into a single overwhelming bloom of night-fire-red, ember-orange, and ritual-gold filling the entire frame; the breakdown drains this to one slow-pulsing ember before the final build rebuilds it layer by layer.
filmPrompt: Sweeping crane shot rising high above the entire fire circle at its most intense, smoke and sparks filling the air, the camera holds steady through the breakdown's stillness before the final surge of motion, no faces.
imagePrompt: A wide elevated view of a fire circle at its most intense, smoke, sparks, and gold dust filling the night air above dozens of drums catching firelight, night-fire-red glow saturating the scene, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, climactic and respectful, no visible faces.

title: Smoke Still Rising
description: The ensemble thins back toward its origins as a slow conga pattern and a soft deep house bass carry the last of the dancers, the energy settling like smoke after the fire's peak.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (full but softened percussion carried over from the peak) -> Section A (toms and shaker drop away, congas and bass remain) -> Breakdown (conga solo, pad swell, bass fades to a hum) -> Section B (frame drum rejoins gently) -> Outro (conga and frame drum alone, bass gone, fading slowly)
soundDesignPrompt: Congas carry the primary rhythmic weight here, played softer and slower in character than during the peak phase; a single frame drum rejoins gently partway through as a callback to the album's opening voice; bass softens into a warm, low hum rather than a driving pulse; pad becomes more prominent, a long cinematic exhale.
mixMasterNotes: Shift the mix balance to favor warmth and space over punch — let the pad breathe more than it has all album, soften percussion transients slightly to suit the settling energy, ensure the bass hum stays felt rather than driving.
djTransitionNotes: First track of the closing phase — works well mixed down from track 10's energy with a slow filter pull; 120 BPM gives room to either continue descending into track 12 or hold steady for one more lower-energy moment.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, softened conga lead, returning frame drum callback, warm low bass hum, long cinematic pad exhale, 120 BPM, settling reflective descending mood, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: The dense gold-and-red bloom from the peak slowly unravels into thin trailing threads of ember-orange smoke against a darkening night-fire-red backdrop; one small ritual-gold point of light (the returning frame drum) reappears and drifts upward alone.
filmPrompt: Slow pull-back shot as the fire circle empties, smoke trails climbing straight into still air, firelight dimming gradually, lingering on glowing embers rather than motion, no faces.
imagePrompt: A fire circle settling after its peak, embers glowing low, thin smoke trails rising straight into a dark night sky, scattered drums resting quietly on packed earth, deep earth-brown tones overtaking the fading night-fire-red glow, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, calm and respectful.

title: Last Ember, First Light
description: The album closes where it began — a single frame drum, now joined briefly by the full ensemble in memory before returning to solo, as the coals dim toward dawn.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (solo frame drum, mirroring track 1's opening) -> Section A (soft pad and distant accordion texture bloom briefly) -> Memory Build (faint echoes of conga and tom patterns from earlier tracks layer in softly) -> Peak Memory (brief, gentle recall of the full ensemble at low volume) -> Release (ensemble fades, frame drum and pad remain) -> Outro (frame drum alone, final strike, silence)
soundDesignPrompt: Returns to the solo frame drum from track 1, now joined briefly by soft, low-volume echoes of the conga, tom, and shaker patterns from across the album, treated with longer reverb tails to feel like memory rather than presence; bass is reduced to a faint, warm sub presence; pad and a single distant accordion phrase close the album with the same restraint as the Portuguese guitar moment in track 3, appearing once and dissolving.
mixMasterNotes: Master for a gentle, intimate close — wide dynamic range, soft transients, generous reverb tails on the percussion echoes to create distance and memory; the final frame drum strike should be the last sound, clean and unaccompanied, mirroring the album's opening.
djTransitionNotes: Natural closing track for a set or the album itself; long, quiet intro and outro make it ideal for a slow fade to silence rather than a hard mix; if continuing a set, only suitable to lead into a complete change of energy or genre.
stylePrompt: Tribal house, solo closing frame drum, soft reverberant echoes of congas toms and shaker, faint warm sub bass, distant single accordion phrase, cinematic pad, 119 BPM, circular farewell mood, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: The single ember-gold point from track 1 returns, now joined briefly by faint, translucent echoes of every shape and color used across the album, layered softly and semi-transparent like afterimages, before all but the one gold point fade to black as dawn-blue creeps in at the frame's edge.
filmPrompt: Slow static shot on dying embers as the sky just begins to lighten at the horizon, smoke thinning to nothing, a single hand resting still on a frame drum, final strike, then stillness, no faces.
imagePrompt: A single frame drum resting beside nearly dead coals as the first hint of dawn light touches the horizon, faint smoke dissipating into still air, deep earth-brown and fading night-fire-red giving way to soft pale light, cinematic painterly realism, square composition, quiet and respectful, no visible faces.
